entry.title: Re: Using a BAPI or functional module to add a catalog to...
entry.content: Also one blog post by SAP: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/fiori-for-s-4hana-mass-maintenance-of-business-roles-for-sap-fiori/ba-p/13366973

entry.title: How to make a result bold in ALV
entry.content:
Hi there! I want to display a result in ALV in bold style if the case was successed. The result that I'm talking about is ls_alv_line because it contain the result. 

Thank you

 CASE ls_grp_nature-id_groupe.
        WHEN 1.
          CASE ls_nature-id_nature.
            WHEN '1'.
              CLEAR ls_alv_line.
              ls_alv_line-id_groupe        = ls_grp_nature-id_groupe.
              ls_alv_line-id_nature        = ls_nature-id_nature.
              ls_alv_line-description      = ls_nature-description.

              ls_alv_line-exercice = ls_cpc_line-total_ex.
              ls_alv_line-exercice_pre = ls_cpc_line-total_ex_prec.
              APPEND  ls_alv_line TO et_esg_alv. CASE ls_grp_nature-id_groupe.
        WHEN 1.
          CASE ls_nature-id_nature.
            WHEN '1'.
              CLEAR ls_alv_line.
              ls_alv_line-id_groupe        = ls_grp_nature-id_groupe.
              ls_alv_line-id_nature        = ls_nature-id_nature.
              ls_alv_line-description      = ls_nature-description.

              ls_alv_line-exercice = ls_cpc_line-total_ex.
              ls_alv_line-exercice_pre = ls_cpc_line-total_ex_prec.
              APPEND  ls_alv_line TO et_esg_alv.

entry.title: Drag and Drop Functionality for Attachments using RAP
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I'm looking for a drag and drop functionality using RAP where users can drag multiple files and upload at one shot. Please let me know the solution or suggestions here if anyone has achieved this kind of functionality.

Thanks,

entry.title: SAP Screen Personas Installation
entry.content:
We have been working towards installing the screen personas add-on in one of our sandbox environments for exploration and demo purposes. 

When our basis team did the original installation, it seems like they did not install the correct support pack versions, so now when I look at the installed product versions, it just has SP00 listed. I noticed this when I first tried accessing the /personas/admin screen and the UI looked really outdated.

What is the best approach for getting this up-to-date? Can we just use the SPAM/SAINT process to install all of the support packs up to SP19?

Our basis team also provided us this error they encountered when attempting to continue that process:

Appreciate any feedback or guidance on how to proceed.

entry.title: SAP MII BLS Transactions alternative in SAP DM (Digital Manufacturing)
entry.content:
Hi,

@ManoelCosta @salvatore_castro and All SAP DM experts,

While we are transforming MII to DM (Digital Manufacturing) one area is how DM can help in replacing existing highly customized MII transactions/BLS where lots of ZBAPIs RFCs are used and data is uploaded downloaded to and from ECC, SQL etc.? DM talks about Serive Registry but in that case we will have to still use MII. What after MII end of life and complete replacement of MII?

I could not find any straight forward solution for this. Please help with the the details.

Thanks and regards

entry.title: Re: Fiori App: Manage Sales Order V2 does not start due t...
entry.content: show me what you have activated

entry.title: Water Track at the SAP for Energy and Utilities Conference 2025: Feedback and Lessons Learnt
entry.content:
As a result of the policy to provide a dedicated track for Water and Wastewater Services use cases initiated at the International SAP for Utilities Conference 2023 in Basel, it is becoming a tradition to meet a significant number of water and wastewater services companies interested on innovative strategies and technologies to drive digital transformation in their companies.

Source: TAC Insights mosaic
Have a look at the detailed agenda.

This year the agenda of the track was centered in the gain of operational efficiencies by providing three inspirational cases

  • SAP for Water and Wastewater Services, Building the Resilient Water Company by Miquel Carbo, SAP
  • ArcGIS by Esri on HANA: Building Next Generation of Integrated Asset Management by Inge Opreel, Farys and Niels van der Vaart, ESRI
  • Streamlining Service Operations: SAP Field Service Management (FSM) Integration at Vitens by Marion Eijkelkamp and  Krijn Verdaasdonk, Vitens

Together with this dedicated track, the Conference offered additional water-specific or water-relevant conferences before and after the Water Track,  configuring an entire agenda specifically for delegates of this industry:

  • Latest Innovations in SAP Asset Management (Asset Management track)
  • Business Data Cloud – Unlocking the Power of Unified Data
  • SAP Asset Performance Management (APM) at AkerBP:  From Test Environment to Production
  • Unlocking the Potential of AI with SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and Datasphere (SAP Microforum at 10:30am
  • A Day in a Life of a Water Drop: Deep Dive into the Benefits of Asset Management at the Lifecycle of a Waterdrop (session needs to be pre-booked via the app)
  • Transformation from OnPrem to SAP RISE: Lessons from Andel’s Journey (AI Track)

First slot, entitled “SAP for Water and Wastewater Services, Building the Resilient Water Company” elaborated around the concept of resilience among Water distribution companies: water systems must be able to withstand and recover from various challenges such as climate change, population growth, and infrastructure challenges. It requires implementing strategies and technologies that ensure a continuous and reliable supply of clean water, even in the face of disruptions.

This includes the use of smart metering platforms, intelligent reporting, and satellite imaging to monitor and manage water resources effectively. By building resilience, government/water companies can enhance their capacity to adapt to changing conditions, protect public health, and support sustainable development.

Farys' use case story entitled  “Transforming Water Management with SAP and GIS Integration”, provide attendees an opportunity to gain insights on how Farys optimizes water management by integrating ArcGIS Utility Network with SAP S/4HANA, creating a seamless connection between GIS and enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems. This successful collaboration demonstrates how utilities can streamline asset management, optimize field operations, and enable data-driven decision-making. Explore the tangible benefits Farys achieved, from enhanced operational efficiency to smarter spatial insights, paving the way for innovation in utility management:

  • Benefits of integrating GIS and ERP systems
  • Focus on asset management
  • Tools explored to enhance operations and decision-making

Vitens use case “Streamlining Service Operations: SAP Field Service Management (FSM) Integration” brought a great story about

How Vitens, the primary water supplier of the Netherlands,  successfully transformed its work order scheduling and management for, at this moment, 240 technicians and more to come to serve for 6 million Dutch inhabitants and commercial customers, maintaining a total of 2.7 million connections, 48.000 km pipeline network and 93 production facilities.

 

During the presentation of their SAP Field Service Management (FSM) implementation, Vitens addressed the challenges of outdated systems and delivered a modern, user-friendly planning tool tailored to its diverse departmental needs. The solution, integrated with S/4HANA, ensures efficient planning, enhanced user experience, and robust support for planners and technicians alike. Main points of the presentation were:

 

  • How SAP FSM is being used across different Vitens' departments, adapting to varied planning methods.
  • Implementation effort and challenges
  • Process improvement achievements to ensure uninterrupted service and optimal resource allocation

 

 

Overall, the collaboration and networking among attendees, this is utility companies and SAP partners has raised again the level of expectations of delegates. Feedback received by Water and Waste Water companies has been very positive on the high quality inspirational speeches and the facilitation of fluent and interesting networking to a level that makes very difficult but a motivating challenge to keep for next year!

entry.title: Re: Run Foreign Trade Regulations Z4
entry.content: Hello, I assume this relates to SAP S/4HANA, because there's no customizing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition using the IMG. Best regards, Henrike

entry.title: The committed amount is not updated when a purchase order or purchase requisition is rejected.
entry.content:
In the case where a purchase order is rejected in the flexible workflow, the committed amount is not updated. 

Is there a way to update automatically the committed amount  when the task of the workflow is rejected?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: The committed amount is not updated when a purchase o...
entry.content:
Reason for system behavior and Possible work around available as of now if decided to not resubmit the Rejected document and expect the Budget / Commitment to be released or updated is  updated in below KBA

3607859 - Update of Committed Amount When a Purchase Order or Purchase Requisition is Rejected - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: Handle persons leaving the organisation when provisioning with IPS
Re: Handle persons leaving the organisation when provisio...
entry.content:
I am now trying to combine user active and membership of the group
 
So
 {
                "condition": "$.active == false",
                "constant": false,
                "targetPath": "$.active"
            },
 
If user set inactive in IAS, it remains inactive through the transformation
 
                "condition": "$.active != false && $.memberOf[?(@ == 's4s_qas ')] empty true",
                "constant": false,
                "targetPath": "$.active"
            },
 
If user set active and group s4s_qas is not assigned, $.active needs to be false
 
 
            {
                "condition": "$.active != false && $.memberOf[?(@ == 's4s_qas ')] empty false",
                "constant": true,
                "targetPath": "$.active"
            },
 
If user set active and group s4s_qas is  assigned, $.active needs to be true
 
 
 
I tried all kinds of options here, but each time my users are not processed. logging shows users are read, but write does not occur. 
 
Hope anyone can help here

Hi Junwu,

many thanks for your help... it worked out.

I have also activated the Navigation Target Apps and now it is working... At least in a way it was possible, since this OData Service is not available in our System: /SSB/SMART_BUSINESS_RUNTIME_SRV

BR,

Milan

entry.title: More then one object for multilangual content in SAP SuccessFactors Learning
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I want to find a way on how we can share Mandatory annual trainings in an efficient way with as little room for errors as possible.

Right now, we create one item for each language and put them in a curriculum requirement to be distributed via the Curricula and an assignment profile. That works, but there were quite a few human-errors in the past.

I know, that it is possible in an item to include the same content in different languages and choose in the setting, that the item gets moved to the learning history, if one content ( = one language) is completed.

HOWEVER, I did not see an option where I can do it this way when I have several content objects with different languages. For example I'd have the following content that is relevant to the training:

 

Chapter 1 - GermanChapter 1 - English
Chapter 2 - GermanChapter 2 - English

Assuming that the chapters can not be merged obviously.

Does someone have an idea how to handle the rollout in a lean way?

Thank you so much!

SAP SuccessFactors Learning

entry.title: Issue with Adapt UI in Fiori Launchpad
entry.content:
I tried using the Adapt UI feature from the Fiori Launchpad. (The button appears after assigning SAP_UI_FLEX_KEY_USER to my user), but after clicking it:

  • The Adapt UI toolbar loads, but nothing is editable.

  • No UI elements get highlighted when hovering.

  • I can't right-click or select any fields for editing.

  • It just stays on the shell header/navigation — no interaction with the actual app content

It seems like the app doesn’t support runtime adaptation

Let me know if anyone has encountered this or if we have a recommended workaround.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Automatic Requisition update based on Position field changes
entry.content:
Hello,

Can anyone please suggest, how can we exclude the Closed job Req for auto updating position deatils, we just want for Approved Reqs only. we tried to remove Internal Status Closed= 2 But when we run the job, it is throwing and error and also tried to add Closed in filter but still it didn't worked. Is there any way where we can exclude the Closed/ Cancelled job Req auto updating?

 

Thankyou!

entry.title: Re: 在管理付款申请APP页面，执行付款策略时，选择了银行之后，无法选择金融交易类型，导致付款申请无法下推，不能生成付款凭证
entry.content:
Machine translation:

On the Manage Payment Request app page, when executing the payment strategy, once the bank is selected, the financial transaction type cannot be selected, causing the payment request to be pushed down and the payment document cannot be generated

1. In the Manage Payment Request app page, when executing the payment strategy, the financial transaction type cannot be selected after the bank has been selected, which prevents payment requests from being pushed down and payment documents cannot be generated; 2. If the financial transaction type for generating the cash flow statement is cancelled, the correct cash flow statement cannot be generated; how to process it

entry.title: Re: Connect with SAP Community at SAP Sapphire and Share Your Special SAP Event Moment!
entry.content: @Qualiture Those were the best of times Ro !!! We are still looking forward to hosting you guys in the US 🙂 Give Mo and the kids our love !!! You are the best and don't be naughty 😉

entry.title: How to import existing on-premises application from ABAP repository in SAP BAS?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have a question regarding working with on-premise systems in SAP Business Application Studio (BAS).

In SAP Web IDE, we used to connect our on-premise system through destinations configured via the SAP Cloud Connector. Then, we would use the ABAP repository option to search for an existing application by its technical name, import it into our workspace, make the necessary changes, and deploy it back to the system.

Is it possible to follow the same process in SAP BAS? If yes, could someone please explain the steps or share a guide on how to import an existing on-premise application from the ABAP repository into BAS, make changes, and deploy it back to the on-premise system?

I have gone through several blogs related to how to migrate projects from SAP Web IDE to BAS. However, I haven’t come across any blog specifically explaining how to use the same on-premise application import and deploy process in BAS like we do in Web IDE.

It would be really helpful if someone could provide resources, a detailed guide, or share their experience on this topic.

Thanks in advance!

Thanks, 

Prasad Reddy

entry.title: Re: Automatic Requisition update based on Position field changes
entry.content:
Hi @Rucha30 , in my post above from 2023 March 08, you can see how we built it into our business rule. Instead of excluding Closed, we only included Open and On Hold job reqs. That way our Closed reqs and any that are Pending don't get updated. We didn't want Pending reqs to update, because we have multiple approvers of job reqs, and if the data on the job req changes midway through the approval process, that's bad for auditing. We also figured if the req is only Pending, then the recruiter can just cancel the req and start over with good data from the Position.

Hope that helps!

[Edited to add:] If you're talking about the Integration Center job, in my same post above you can see the screenshot where we included a filter for Approved, so that might be what you are missing.

entry.title: SAP RAP: Create Order with Business Partner
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm fairly new to RAP and would like to know what's the best way to implement the following scenario.

I would like to create an OData service for S/4Hana which is able to create an order and a business partner via deep create for example a customer or contact person.

I have found the Business Object Interface for the Business Partner "I_BusinessPartnerTP_2" which I can hope I can integrate into my service. I have also created a simple RAP service that is able to create the order as an unmanaged scenario.

Now I'm not sure how to associate my existing service with the Business Object Interface so I can create the order and the business partner together.

Thanks a lot

Lennart

entry.title: Re: SAP MII BLS Transactions alternative in SAP DM (Digit...
entry.content:
Hello @khaleelurrehman_badeghar and thanks for the post.

Without knowing too much about your customizations and what you're able to achieve at a business process level, and for this I highly recommend leveraging our SAP Customer Evolution Kit for Manufacturing.

That said, I can still provide some insight into the technical components to map over the processes into SAP Digital Manufacturing Production Process Designer.  For this to work you have to first wrap your ZBAPIs into one or multiple Enterprise Services (depends on what you're doing) and there are videos that I have seen in the past on this and also core SAP Enterprise Service Help documentation available.  Once you have the WSDLs created for these services you can map them into SAP Digital Manufacturing via the Manage Service Registry app.  Once their definition is mapped into here you can then consume it as a "block" in the production process designer flow.

Hope this helps,

Sam

entry.title: Re: Automatic Generation of Packaging item for Goods issue S4 HANA
entry.content:
@harshnama 

Please check what is the delivery type you have used for the delivery creation for that delivery type you have to assign item category VERP and item usage PACK then default item category HUPM

entry.title: Re: Original print and reprint PO
entry.content: I have found if the MRP Controller field is "Blank" the reprint option is not permitted.

entry.title: Re: How to introduce new MVC Interceptor to OCC extension?
entry.content:
In your custom occ extension , you can use a HandlerInterceptor : 

@Component

public class CustomHandlerInterceptor implements HandlerInterceptor {

    @Override
    public boolean preHandle(HttpServletRequest request, HttpServletResponse response, Object handler) {
        //your logic here
    }
}

And then add your HandlerInterceptor to the InterceptorRegistry using a WebMvcConfigurer (always in your occ extension) : 

@Configuration

public class OccWebConfig implements WebMvcConfigurer {

    @Autowired
    private CustomHandlerInterceptor customHandlerInterceptor;

    @Override
    public void addInterceptors(InterceptorRegistry registry) {
        registry.addInterceptor(customHandlerInterceptor)
                .addPathPatterns("/**");
    }
}

Make sure that your customocc-web-spring.xml is scanning the HandlerInterceptor and the WebMvcConfigurer packages (<context:component-scan base-package="com.project.occ"/>)

entry.title: Document not shown in Generic Object Sevices on delivery item level
entry.content:
Hi all,

using ArchiveLink we have a reference to a document in the Archive-System. The link is on item level in the delivery. We see an entry in Link-Table TOA02 and can access the archived document using transaction OAER.

The document was created using output control on item level. Customizing of output type and Archive link seem ok.

But we cannot access the document from delivery item level, it is not shown in the Generic Object Sevices (Attachment list).

What is missing?
Kind regards Elko

entry.title: Re: Validation Rule - Compare Opening balances to Prior y...
entry.content:
Hi Martin,

Thank you - I saw the help too earlier.

My question is re 2023 OP.

Previously I tested in the 2502 cloud version selecting YTD for previous year and as you noted it selects the parallel prior year period (e.g. 2025.06 vs. 2024.06) instead of 2025.0 vs. 2024.12). I accessed the 2023 OP FPS01 version (we are currently migrating from EC-CS to GR so I can't test yet) and did not see any option to further limit to period 12.
How do you limit - Where' to the YTD calculation. The the rule should then validate YTD up to period 12 for the previous year."?
Regards,
Eyal

entry.title: Re: How to make a result bold in ALV
entry.content: ALV for Web Dynpro?

entry.title: Re: Understanding Crystal Report Connections
entry.content: CR doesn't rwecommend using multiple Commands to access the same database. If you must use a subreport per Command

entry.title: Where are Time Account Rules maintained and configured?
entry.content:
For Time Administration, where are Time Account Rules maintained and configured?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Where are Time Account Rules maintained and configured?
entry.content:
Where are Time Account Rules maintained and configured?

  1. Go to Business Configuration work center.
  2. Enter the Overview view.
  3. Search for Time Accounts.
  4. Highlight the version for the relevant country and Change Immediately.
  5. Under the header Time Account Rules select Maintain Time Account Rules.
  6. Find the relevant Time Account Type.

entry.title: Re: Pl Deliver time time option in external service PR no...
entry.content:
Hello @ajit2804 ,

Please refer the MRP2 view of the material in the material master. Check if the planned delivery time is maintained or not. This planned delivery time gets copied into the PR.

 

Vaibhav Mishra

entry.title: Re: Problem with my SAP Fiori Elements app
entry.content: Hi Radoslaw_kiela2 Following your advice, I checked the Error_LOG by entering the query that appeared in the console, and indeed, it showed a problem with "SAP_CreatedDateTime" The property 'SAP_CreatedDateTime' has an invalid value of '739379.0000000' What do you recommend doing next? Checking something else to get more context in this case?

entry.title: Re: Problem with my SAP Fiori Elements app
entry.content: Hi Junwu I checked the Error_LOG by entering the query that appeared in the console, and indeed, it showed a problem with "SAP_CreatedDateTime" The property 'SAP_CreatedDateTime' has an invalid value of '739379.0000000' What do you recommend doing next? Should I check something else to get more context in this case? Thanks EBZ

entry.title: Re: Alphanumeric number ranges for Equipment in Plant Mai...
entry.content:
Hello @Prasenjit91 ,

Please put a deletion flag on the non-relevant equipment. Then enhance the drop-down list in the notification and order creation screen by removing the non-relevant equipment from drop down list. If you create a new one, then tracking the maintenance history will become a challenge for end users. Even if equipment is old, history is still relevant. Recreation of equipment with a different number range looks good, but from the viewpoint of tracking maintenance history, it is not a good idea.

Vaibhav

entry.title: Re: Translation Problem in Adobe Form
entry.content: It is 2025, and this solution still works. 😄

entry.title: Question: Versioning logic in BrokerDetail
entry.content:
Dear SAP APM and Advanced Workflow expert, 

We have the following requirements:

A client has a workflow called “Profile Changes,” where different users update Agent or ATM Profiles. The client now wants to:

  1. Add functionality to this workflow to allow upline (territory) modifications.
  2. Automate the versioning process and ensure the impacted records in BrokerDetail are versioned accordingly.

    We have two possible solutions for the versioning logic:

    • Developing it in APMe using QBQuery
    • Implementing it within Advanced Workflow

      Please advise which solution you recommend and why?

      SAP SuccessFactors Agent Performance Management Advanced Workflow

entry.title: SAC outer join of two models
entry.content:
Hi all,

There is a need to compare data of two models (both with measure and w/o account). As result only those records of model A should be shown that do not match model B. How it can be developed?

P.S. I have found options that might help, but I cannot find them in the system:

‘All primary data’ – similar to left outer join

‘All data’ – similar to full outer join

‘Intersecting data only’ – similar to inner join

Best regards,

Oleksandr

entry.title: Re: SF LMS Event Based Integration
entry.content:
Hi @MelanieBelzile , yes we use Intelligent Services in our integration.

In SF LMS Admin we've configured the Service event bus (flag sebPublishedEventTypes[LearningCompletion]=true) and have set up in Intelligent Services an "Event Connector". Within the Event Connector you define the endpoint where the event message shall be delivered to. In our setup we've chosen SAP CPI.

Here, we receive the event, read "total hours" from SF LMS via SF LMS OData API and map all the information for the target application. From the target application the CPI iFlow receives the response if delivery is/was successful and passes it to SF.

From my experience this integration was pretty slim and is state-of-the-art. And its (almost) real-time (2 sec. from SF LMS to target system).

entry.title: Re: Custom field with multiline in S4 public cloud-key us...
entry.content: thanks Prashant. Even if i resize, when i edit it, it gives works like one line inout field

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP ABAP environment integration with ChaRM .

Very useful post.

Quick question - is it possible to link directly to the "View All" screen of the respective document(s)?

Is there a definitions list for the parameters (bo, node, operation, etc. etc.) that we can use as a reference document for the different options available?

Many thanks,

Phil

entry.title: Re: Custom field with multiline in S4 public cloud-key us...

entry.title: FINS_ACDOC_POST133 House Bank still assigned to wrong G/L sub account
entry.content:
FINS_ACDOC_POST133 House Bank still assigned to wrong G/L sub account

New G/L Accounts for Bank Accounts

As starting point we created nee G/L Accounts for showing in more detail the related bank accounts in the balance sheet.

  • 11001900 Raiffeisen Bank Account – HUF
  • 11001901 Raiffeisen Bank Account – EUR
  • 11001902 Raiffeisen Bank Account – USD

For example:

 

and the related bank transfer subaccounts.

11001920 Raiffeisen Bank Transfer – HUF

11001921 Raiffeisen Bank Transfer - EUR

11001922 Raiffeisen Bank Transfer – USD

For example:

 

 

New Bank Accounts

Then we created accordingly the new bank account master data.

For example:

 

Upload Bank Statement

Now I want to upload the bank statement. However, for posting the statement an error message shows up.

FINS_ACDOC_POST133

 

Analysis

I reviewed the following

Note: 3376166 - House bank & & is not assigned to G/L account & in company code & (FINS_ACDOC_POST133) - SAP for Me

Blog: How to use Bank Reconciliation Account in Payment ... - SAP Community

This is the configuration in SSCUI 101024 but I can´t figure out how to adjust the settings in order to post to 11001920 instead of 11001080.

 

What do I need to adjust for changing the accounting determination subject to the new subaccounts created?

Many thanks

Sebastian

entry.title: Explore Our Signature Offerings: Access Essential Product Information
entry.content:
Explore Our Signature Offerings: Access Essential Product Information

SAP Sapphire is the premier flagship event series to learn about SAP's strategic vision, new innovations, and industry-leading solutions in action. In Anja Schneider's (SVP & Global Head of Premium Engagements and Advisory, Customer Services & Delivery, SAP) Spotlight Sessions in Orlando and Madrid you will discover how SAP MaxAttention and RISE with SAP turn strategy into action through cloud adoption, AI at scale, and continuous transformation. Anja will also illustrate how such offerings connect data, applications, and business AI to drive innovation—helping leading companies cut infrastructure costs, accelerate go-lives, and transform board-level decision-making. This blog post will provide you with all the necessary links to navigate through our signature offerings SAP Business Data Cloud, SAP Business Accelerator Hub, SAP MaxAttention as well as how you can make the most of SAP MaxAttention’s assistance regarding AI, enabling you to take action right away.

SAP Business Data Cloud

Unlocking the Power of Your Business Data: This is our fully managed SaaS solution that unifies and governs all SAP data and seamlessly connects with third-party data

  • Get an overview and take the product tour
  • Get a free SAP Business Data Cloud Assessment

 


SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Explore our Business Transformation Content & Accelerators designed to support customers’ strategic objectives and industry specific needs alongside our SAP solutions.

  • Central entry point

 

SAP MaxAttention

Find out more about SAP MaxAttention and benefit from a strategic long-term partnership for successful collaboration with SAP.

  • Check out the services and directly request a meeting

 

AI assistance from SAP MaxAttention

  • This introductory video including a demo dashboard will show you what AI assistance from SAP MaxAttention is about.
  • If you are interested in updates on AI assistance from SAP MaxAttention, reach out to us.

entry.title: Join our Live Influencing Session at ASUG Annual Conference in Orlando!
entry.content:
Customer Influence: Shaping the Future of AI-Driven Development with Joule

Be part of the conversation that's shaping the future of development in SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP!

Join SAP experts and your peers for an interactive Live Influencing Session focused on ABAP Cloud, CAP, and the game-changing role of Joule.

✅Gain early insights into AI-driven, cloud-ready development
✅Share your real-world experiences and challenges
✅Collaborate directly with SAP product teams
✅Influence future innovations through live feedback and discussion

Whether you're hands-on with ABAP Cloud, building on SAP BTP, or exploring how AI can accelerate development, this session is your chance to make your voice heard and shape what comes next.

Date: Wednesday, May 21
Time: 2:00–2:40 PM
Location: ASUG Hub – Theater 1 (on the show floor)

Add it to your ASUG Annual Conference agenda – and help influence SAP’s products!

entry.title: How to Configure Cross-Billing Cycle Aggregation for Tier-Based Pricing in SAP Subscription Billing
entry.content:
Subscription businesses often rely on usage-based pricing models to provide flexibility to customers while aligning revenue with actual consumption. In SAP Subscription Billing, tier-based pricing is a powerful mechanism for incentivizing higher usage through volume-based discounts. And when pricing needs to consider usage over a longer period, such as a quarter, cross-billing cycle aggregation becomes invaluable.

This post demonstrates how to implement cross-billing cycle aggregation in SAP Subscription Billing to reflect a common subscription pricing scenario. The example is based on a use case from the SAP Subscription Billing Pricing Configuration Guide, providing practical steps for configuring tier-based pricing.

 

Business Scenario: Tier-Based Pricing for X-Ray Machine Usage

Let’s consider the following use case:

A company that manufactures X-ray machines supplies them to hospitals under a subscription model. Hospitals pay monthly for the machines based on the number of X-ray images. However, prices per image decrease as overall usage increases over a three-month period (quarterly aggregation). This is a classic tier-based pricing structure, designed to encourage higher utilization by offering lower prices for higher consumption volumes.

Here’s the quarterly tiered pricing for X-ray images:

  • Up to 500 images per quarter: $25 per image
  • 501 to 1000 images per quarter: $20 per image
  • Above 1000 images per quarter: $15 per image

To achieve this pricing, we need to implement cross-billing cycle aggregation for a three-month period in SAP Subscription Billing. This ensures usage is tracked and aggregated across the defined period, determining the appropriate tier for billing within each month.

 

Configuring Cross-Billing Cycle Aggregation

In this section, we’ll explore step-by-step how to configure the cross-billing cycle aggregation logic:

Step 1: Configure the Cross-Billing Cycle Aggregation Period

In the rate plan template, specify a cross-billing cycle aggregation period of three months for the usage-based charge. This setup enables the system to track cumulative consumption over three months, resetting the aggregated value at the end of each period. You find these settings on the detail screen of a usage-based charge. Note that this is only supported for rate element with the "Aggregated" rating variant.

 

Step 2: Use Cumulative Quantity in the Pricing Logic

The cumulative consumption across the aggregation period is available in a standard pricing field called cumulativeQuantity. Prerequisite is that the charge in the rate plan template is configured to aggregate usage across billing cycles, otherwise the field is not available. The field is populated automatically with the total quantity of usage valid at the end of the rating period.
Note that if your SAP Subscription Billing tenant was created before the new field was added, you may need to manually add this field using the Manage Field Catalog app.

To correctly apply tier-based pricing based on the cumulative quantity, configure the Pricing Logic to utilize the aggregation data. The key is to use a property with the quantity contribution field type, which combines two elements:

  • The quantity to be priced (e.g., the current rating period's usage)
  • The offset, used to determine the relevant tier.

This field is filled based on the values quantity and cumulativeQuantity as described below, and then used to calculate the amount.

The logic has the following steps:

  • Fetch the price condition
    Use the Table Lookup operator to fetch the appropriate tiered price condition.
  • Calculate the offset
    Since cumulativeQuantity already includes the aggregated quantity from previous periods plus the current rating period's usage, your pricing logic must calculate the offset by subtracting the current period's quantity from the cumulative total. Use the Execute Formula operator to implement this calculation.
  • Define the quantity contribution
    The Define Property operator lets you create the required property type. Use the field quantity (representing the current usage) and the calculated offset as components of the quantity contribution.
  • Calculate the price element
    For tier-based models, the Calculate Price Element operator uses the quantity contribution to calculate the appropriate price. While the scale value parameter is relevant only for volume pricing models, you can directly reference cumulativeQuantity.

Here’s how the final pricing logic for the price element specification looks.

 

Example: Verifying the Setup and Results

Once the pricing logic is set up, validate the configuration by following these steps:

  1. Create a product that references the rate plan template.
  2. Generate a subscription for the customer (for example, a hospital).
  3. Record usage data for the subscription. For instance, input monthly X-ray image counts.

Here’s an example showing usage values over several months and how it impacts the cumulativeQuantity field:

 JanFebMarAprMayJunJul

Quantity (Images)

300

270

350

330

320

260

0

cumulativeQuantity

300

570

920

330

550

810

0

After posting the usage, you can validate the outcome in the charge details of the generated bills. Notably, you’ll see the aggregated consumption for the aggregation period and how the pricing tiers were applied.

Observing the Tiers

  • February bill: With a cumulative quantity of 570 images, the quantity of 270 images is distributed amoung two tiers: 200 images are billed at $25 per image, and the remaining 70 are billed at $20 per image.


 

  • March bill: With a cumulative quantity of 920 images, the final images fall into the $20-per-image tier.



  • April bill: After three months, the cycle resets, and the cumulative quantity starts over at 0 for the next period.

     

Additional Notes

  • Illustrative purposes: While quarterly aggregation is used in this example, SAP Subscription Billing allows you to define other aggregation periods, such as yearly or bi-monthly, depending on your business needs.
  • The periodic reset needs to be a multiple of the billing cycle length. For example, it is not possible to configure a reset period of 3 months for a bi-monthly billing cycle.
  • Beyond periodic resets, SAP Subscription Billing also supports indefinite aggregation, where cumulative values are tracked over the entire subscription lifecycle.

Conclusion

By using cross-billing cycle aggregation in SAP Subscription Billing, businesses can implement robust and flexible pricing models that reflect customer activity over time. For scenarios like quarterly tier-based pricing, this feature ensures accurate billing aligned with real-world consumption patterns and rewards long-term loyalty.

We hope this guide empowers you to design pricing models tailored to your subscription business needs. If you have questions, insights, or additional use cases to share, feel free to leave a comment or start a discussion below. Let’s learn and grow together as a community!

Call to Action

🔍 Looking for more tips and tricks? Check out related blog posts on SAP Community or explore the SAP Subscription Billing Documentation for in-depth guidance.

entry.title: Re: ISLM embedded scenario in public cloud
entry.content: Custom CDS view entity needs to release. then only it will show in training dataset area. package issue i think it's a new version update.

entry.title: Re: 1H 2025 Onboarding: Enhancements to the E-Verify Further Action Notice process
entry.content:
Certainly! I've also updated my blog to better refer to them above.

The new triggers are:

(ONB) E-Verify Further Action Notice Requires New Hire Action Trigger
(ONB) E-Verify Further Action Notice Completed by New Hire Trigger


The new notification templates are:

(ONB) Further Action Notice Requires New Hire Action Template
(ONB) Confirm New HIre's Tentative Nonconfirmation Response Template

entry.title: How to automatically block for payment a document reopened during FF.5 bank statement import?
entry.content:
Olá Comunidade!

Hello Community!

I'm facing a functional challenge in the Financial Accounting (FI) module of SAP S/4HANA 2023 On-Premise, and I’d appreciate your help!

I'm trying to implement a solution where documents that are automatically reopened during the bank statement import via transaction FF.5 are automatically blocked for payment.

Scenario:

The bank sends, within the statement, a payment rejection instruction.
During the execution of FF.5, based on the configuration in OT83, the system reverses the clearing and reopens the original document.

The business team would like this reopened document not to be picked up again automatically by the F110S payment job, until it is manually reviewed to confirm whether the payment should actually be made.

Objective:

To prevent inadvertently re-paying documents reopened by FF.5, by automatically applying a payment block (ZLSPR) during the FF.5 execution.

My question:

Is there any BAdI, user-exit, or configuration that allows documents reopened via FF.5 to be automatically blocked for payment?

Any guidance is much appreciated!

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Problem with my SAP Fiori Elements app
entry.content: you have to ask yourself why it has that value?

entry.title: Re: 1H 2025 Onboarding: Enhancements to the E-Verify Further Action Notice process
entry.content: @Katie_L can you try, for example, 555-68-5511? You can then increase the last digit 1-6 to get the various scenarios to test out.

entry.title: Re: SAP Screen Personas Installation
entry.content:
It would be recommended to use SUM / Maintenance Planner to do the upgrade, but yes, you can do this via SPAM and install all the missing support packages. Be sure to also include the ACP called PERSONAS==300 in the queue, to avoid potential problems during the process.

Once done, implement all the Personas notes according to the Health Check and you'll be good to go.

that are not going to sapphire but rather hang around here and trying to catch up?

cheers,

gm

entry.title: Re: Future dated onboardees cannot be searched via Inacti...
entry.content:
Hi  ,

Thank you for your follow-up. I understand that you're unable to locate the specific permission under Employee Data in Role-Based Permissions (RBP) to enable searching for inactive employees. Based on the information provided and the available resources, here's the exact permission you need to enable:

Required Permission:

  • Permission Name: Include Inactive Employees in the search
  • Location: Admin Center > Manage Permission Roles > [Select Role] > Permissions > Manage Hires or Manage Recruits

Enabling this permission allows users with the assigned role to search for inactive employees, including future-dated hires, within the Employee Profile.

Additional Considerations:

  • Global Search Header Visibility: If the "Hide External Users from Search Results" option is enabled in Company System and Logo Settings, future-dated hires will not appear in the global search header. However, they can still be searched within the Employee Profile if the above permission is granted.
  • Role Assignment: Ensure that the permission is enabled for the appropriate roles (e.g., HR, Admin) and that these roles are assigned to the relevant users.
  • Testing: After configuring the permissions, test the functionality by searching for a future-dated hire within the Employee Profile to confirm that the permissions are correctly applied.

If you continue to experience issues or need further assistance, please provide additional details about your current configuration, and I'll be glad to help you troubleshoot further.

Thanks,

kr,

karthick.

entry.title: Re: No data is available for the chart to render. SAC
entry.content:
Its a Live connection, check for variable change in the back end. If need to maintain appropriate values to those variables maintain them. 

Check for Filters applied on this widget and adjust if necessary to display your data.

Correct if any overlapping  filters that restricts the data.

In analyzer see the data with the same measures/dimensions in your widget and Create a test widget with same set of designer controls and see if the data coming there and you can easily understand the reason why data is not showing here.

Hope this helps.

entry.title: Regional and Local Premium Engagements Events
entry.content:
Regional and Local Premium Engagements Events

We are delighted to inform you about the upcoming regional and local Premium Engagements event series that provides invited customers with a unique opportunity to gain first-hand insights from SAP experts on key topics such as SAP Business Suite, SAP Business Data Cloud, SAP Business AI, RISE with SAP, clean core, and enterprise architecture. Each one- or two-day event offers a deep dive into key topics covered at the SAP MaxAttention Summit and demonstrates the central role SAP MaxAttention and premium engagements plays in the success of SAP’s top customers.

Attendees will be given extensive opportunities to network and will have the chance to share their experiences with like-minded peers in the Premium Engagements community. The events feature keynotes and breakout sessions with SAP executives, experts, and customers, allowing attendees to engage with each other and gain valuable insights from industry professionals.

Please find an overview of all currently announced regional and local Premium Engagements Forums 2025 below. Updates will continuously be added to the respective pages.

  • APAC (GC): July 9 in Beijing
  • APAC (APJ): August 26-28 in Kuala Lumpur
  • APAC (ANZ): September 4-5 in Sydney
  • AMERICAS (NA): October 28-29 in NSQ
  • AMERICAS (Mexico): November 4 in Mexico City
  • AMERICAS (Brazil): November 6-7 in Sao Leopoldo

Save-the-date emails and event invites will be sent to all Premium Engagements customers. We encourage you to register and reserve a spot in your calendar as soon as you receive your invitation.

For our Market Units in EMEA and MEE there will also be a few smaller events with the first events kicking off in June.

EMEA

  • Madrid, June 12
  • Paris, June 19
  • Milan, June 26
  • Johannesburg, July 8
  • Cape Town, July 10
  • Dubai, July 16
  • Hertogenbosch, September 11
  • Istanbul, September 25

MEE

  • Heidelberg, June 24
  • Zurich, July 1
  • Vienna, September 24

We look forward to welcoming you to the regional and local events and driving transformation success together. If you have any questions, feel free to post them in the comments below.

entry.title: Re: Connect with SAP Community at SAP Sapphire and Share Your Special SAP Event Moment!
entry.content: @Marssel700 Oh wow, dear friend! Such amazing pictures that bring back great moments! How are you? How have you been?

entry.title: workflow approver does not receive email notification
entry.content:
Hello experts,

We're implementing EC for a client and we've just configured workflows, but the approver does not receive email notification (the template is enabled in email notification template settings). Are there other prerequisites to trigger email notifications from the system?

entry.title: Re: No data is available for the chart to render. SAC
entry.content: Hi 07makita, It looks like the chart isn’t rendering because no data is available at the moment. Since you’re using a live BW connection, sometimes data might not load due to connectivity issues or changes in the BW queries. Here are a few steps you could try: Check the BW query to make sure the key figures are still available and haven’t been modified or deleted. Verify the data source connection in SAC to ensure it’s still active and authorized. Refresh the data manually or try to reload the model in SAC. Confirm that any filters or variables applied in your SAC story aren’t excluding all data by mistake. If all else fails, consider reaching out to your BW admin to check if there are any backend issues or recent changes. Hope this helps! Phonetics tip for pronunciation: SAC — say as “Ess-Ay-See” BW — say as “Bee-Double-You” Good luck fixing it!

entry.title: Re: Message Validation Policy of API Management is not va...
entry.content:
try add <SOAPMessage version= "1.1/1.2" /> to your policy. 

<MessageValidation async="false" continueOnError="false" enabled="true" xmlns="http://www.sap.com/apimgmt">
<Element namespace="urn:sap-com:document:sap:rfc:functions">ZSD_ESALES_TRUCKS_LIST</Element>
<SOAPMessage version= "1.1/1.2" />
<Source>request</Source>
<ResourceURL>wsdl://ZSD_ESALES_TRUCKS_LIST.wsdl</ResourceURL>
</MessageValidation>

entry.title: Re: Connect with SAP Community at SAP Sapphire and Share Your Special SAP Event Moment!
entry.content: Hey @moshenaveh I’m doing well. hope you are too 🙂

entry.title: Re: SAP Inside Track Campinas: knowledge, innovation, and community in action!
entry.content: It was an incredible event!! Lots of learning and a great networking opportunity with our colleagues from the SAP community.

entry.title: Re: EWM PPF Change Output Device
entry.content:
Hi,

If it is a label or form from HU then you can maintain a printer in the condition record in transaction /scwm/gcmc you can also maintain a printer to a user in SU3 that also should work.

If the form is related to delivery or TU you can maintain the printer in /SCWM/SR_PRINT and also in SU3.

entry.title: Hello, let's talk about Business Transformation with LeanIX, Signavio end Walkme?
entry.content: I'm Rodrigo, from São Paulo, Brazil, and I work with Business Transformation Management, supporting the SAP ecosystem with solutions such as LeanIX for Enterprise Architecture, Signavio for process management and mining, and Walkme for digital adoption!

Can you please look this.

Best regards,

Ghous

entry.title: Connect, Automate, and Thrive with 4 SAP BTP Use Cases You Cannot Miss at Sapphire 2025
entry.content:
Mark your calendars—SAP Sapphire 2025 is just around the corner (or you should already have the calendars marked and starting to pack). Sapphire 2025 will showcase SAP's strategic vision with SAP Business Suite, new innovations like Joule Agents, and industry-leading solutions in action. Whether you're seeking strategic insights or hands-on technology demonstrations, Sapphire 2025 promises to deliver value that extends well beyond the event itself.

 

SAP Business Technology Platform: The Backbone of Intelligent Enterprises

In this blog, I'll explore the breakthrough use cases for 2025 that demonstrate SAP's commitment to creating fully integrated business processes. We'll see how the SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) serves as the essential foundation connecting disparate systems, processes, and data flows to deliver transformative business outcomes. Each use case is supported by comprehensive Discovery Center Missions and Business Accelerators designed to fast-track your implementation and maximize value creation.
For attendees joining us in person, our expert-led sessions will provide deep dives on how to get started with SAP BTP. Our specialists will also be available at the SAP BTP for Business Application booth to address your specific business challenges and help architect your connected enterprise vision. You don’t want to miss the following sessions in Orlando to learn more about the SAP BTP use cases and SAP Discovery Center, so add them to your agenda right now.


BTP1562 | Explore business scenarios enabled by SAP BTP that you can implement today
BTP2467 | Mission possible: Accelerate transformation with SAP Discovery Center

 

New Business Technology Platform Use Cases: Unlocking End-to-End Connectivity 

In today's complex business environment, siloed operations and error-prone, fragmented processes create significant barriers to efficiency and growth. The true power of Intelligent Enterprise lies in seamless connectivity across your entire business landscape and utilizing the unified landscape to automate processes and apply insights analysis. Let's explore four new SAP BTP use cases for 2025 that will help your organization get started:

 

Use Case #1: Connect all applications across the business with SAP Integration Suite

• The Challenge: Organizations today operate with complex landscapes spanning cloud, on-premises, and hybrid environments. Critical business processes often span multiple applications, both from SAP and from other vendors. creating fragmentation that leads to inefficiencies and data inconsistencies. These fragmented processes add manual work, increase maintenance costs, and limit real-time access to valuable data.
• How SAP BTP Can Help: SAP Integration Suite provides a comprehensive integration platform that seamlessly connects your entire enterprise ecosystem—from SAP Business Suite applications to third-party solutions, legacy systems, and emerging technologies. The platform's pre-built integration content significantly accelerates implementation time, while its API management capabilities allow you to securely expose business processes to partners and developers.

By creating unified, end-to-end process flows, organizations can eliminate data silos and ensure consistent information across systems. Real-time data monitoring provides visibility into cross-system records, enabling proactive management of business operations. The outcome is a connected business environment where information flows across your enterprise landscape, driving efficiency. CONA Services is a great example of an organization that was able to minimize data errors and increase master data accuracy with SAP Integration Suite.

 

Use Case #2: Drive Your Business with Integrated Enterprise Performance Management

• The Challenge: Financial planning and performance management processes frequently operate in isolation from operational data sources. Finance teams spend excessive time collecting and reconciling data across systems rather than focusing on strategic analysis. This disconnect between financial planning and operational execution creates misalignment between corporate objectives and day-to-day business activities.
• How SAP BTP Can Help: SAP's integrated Enterprise Performance Management solution on BTP creates a seamless connection between financial planning and operational execution. By leveraging SAP BTP capabilities, organizations can assess new opportunities, improve profitability, and respond with greater agility in the face of unexpected change and disruption. It brings together data from Cloud ERP and other business applications to create a unified planning and performance management analysis with SAP Analytics Cloud.

This connected approach enables continuous planning cycles that adapt to changing business conditions. Operational data flows automatically into financial planning models, eliminating manual data collection and reconciliation efforts. Business leaders gain real-time visibility into how operational decisions impact financial outcomes, while predictive capabilities highlight future opportunities and risks. The result is truly connected planning where strategic objectives drive operational execution, and performance insights inform strategic adjustments in a continuous feedback loop. Freudenberg Home and Cleaning Solutions shares how they are aligning their operational planning with financial performance across multiple regions and business functions.

 

Use Case #3: Enhance Employee Onboarding Experience Through Streamlined Processes with AI

• The Challenge: The employee onboarding journey typically requires multiple people across multiple departments and systems—from HR and IT to facilities management and department-specific applications before the employee starts. From using document collection and antiquated paper-based verification to manual data errors, this fragmentation creates a poor experience for the new hire before they’ve even started and reduces productivity during the critical first months, while also increasing administrative burden across the organization. HR teams struggle to orchestrate complex cross-departmental workflows, while managers lack visibility into the onboarding progress.
• How SAP BTP Can Help: SAP BTP enables a revolutionary approach to employee onboarding by connecting previously isolated systems and processes into a unified experience. Through SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition, organizations can create a single-entry point for all onboarding activities, while BTP's workflow management capabilities orchestrate tasks across systems and departments.

The solution connects SAP SuccessFactors with identity management, equipment provisioning, and department-specific processes and applications to create a seamless onboarding journey. New employees experience a coherent process regardless of the underlying systems, while HR teams and managers gain real-time visibility into onboarding progress and can proactively address potential delays. By connecting the entire onboarding ecosystem, organizations significantly reduce time-to-productivity for new hires while decreasing administrative overhead, ultimately transforming onboarding from a series of disconnected tasks to a strategic advantage in talent management. Gerdau S.A. demonstrates how this innovation not only improved the efficiency of the hiring process by leveraging the power of AI to reduce or eliminate manual data entry and review, but also improved the onboarding experience for both candidates and HR.

 

Use Case #4: Unlock Enterprise Automation with the Power of SAP BTP and SAP Signavio

• The Challenge: Business process automation initiatives often focus on isolated processes without considering end-to-end business outcomes. Organizations struggle to identify which processes should be prioritized for automation and how to ensure automated processes remain aligned with business objectives. After implementation, it's difficult to measure the actual impact of automation on business performance.
• How SAP BTP Can Help: The combination of SAP Signavio and SAP BTP creates a powerful solution for intelligent, end-to-end process automation. Signavio's process mining and business process management capabilities enable organizations to visualize current processes, identify bottlenecks, and simulate the impact of potential changes. This intelligence then guides automation implementation through BTP's comprehensive suite of automation tools, including SAP Build Process Automation and integration capabilities.

This integrated approach enables a continuous process improvement cycle: processes are analyzed, optimized, automated, and monitored in a connected loop. Organizations can prioritize automation initiatives based on business impact, implement them seamlessly across integrated systems, and measure the resulting outcomes accurately. By connecting process insights directly to automation implementation, businesses can achieve transformative results such as reduced cycle times, improved compliance, enhanced customer experiences, and significant cost savings while maintaining alignment with strategic objectives. Blue Diamond Growers’ prioritization of business values and elimination of process gaps with the insights from SAP Signavio allowed them to identify the most impactful automation opportunities.

 

“Must-Attend/Watch” SAP BTP Sessions

  • BTP2123 | Unleash the power of your applications, data, and AI with SAP BTP
    Orlando | Virtual
    Speaker: Michael Ameling
  • BTP2293 | Real stories, real impact: How customers are driving success with SAP BTP
    Orlando | Virtual
    Speaker: Bharat Sandhu
  • BTP2122: Explore the Roadmap for SAP Business Technology Platform
    Orlando | Virtual Speaker: Steffen Pietsch
  • BTP1562 | Explore business scenarios enabled by SAP BTP that you can implement today
  • BTP2467 | Mission possible: Accelerate transformation with SAP Discovery Center
  • BTP2075 | Navigate the path to autonomous finance with SAP Business Suite and SAP BTP
  • BTP2072 | Discover how SAP customers are realizing business value with SAP BTP
  • BTP2074 | Maximize your move to SAP cloud ERP and SAP Business Suite with SAP BTP
  • BTP2468 | SAP BTP: The center of a clean core strategy for long-term success

 

To Wrap Up


As we look toward Sapphire 2025, it's clear that end-to-end connectivity is no longer just a technical consideration—it's a strategic imperative for business success. Whether you're focusing on enterprise integration, connected planning, streamlined employee experiences, or intelligent process automation, SAP Business Technology Platform stands as the essential foundation of SAP Business Suite. It connects systems, processes, and experiences across the enterprise landscape, enabling truly integrated business operations.
Visit the SAP Business Technology Platform In Action community page for comprehensive resources on these use cases and join us at Sapphire 2025 to experience firsthand how SAP BTP is redefining what's possible through end-to-end connectivity. Together, we'll explore how your organization can become truly connected, intelligent, and ready for whatever the future holds.

entry.title: Re: Hello, let's talk about Business Transformation with LeanIX, Signavio end Walkme?
entry.content:
Hello Rodrigo,

Very nice to meet you!

Your experience in Business Transformation Management sounds impressive and perfectly aligns with our community's interests. I wanted to let you know about our new Business Transformation Champions group in the SAP Community. It'd be a perfect space for you to share your thoughts and start discussions on anything around transformation and these solution bundle. So please feel free to join the group if you haven't already. 

Additionally, I work as Community Manager for SAP Signavio and SAP LeanIX Communities, so feel free to reach out if you need any assistance. Happy to help 🙂

entry.title: Re: Connect with SAP Community at SAP Sapphire and Share Your Special SAP Event Moment!
entry.content:
Wow, so many great memories!   This inspired me to take a walk down memory lane (of course!).

https://community.sap.com/t5/welcome-corner-blog-posts/i-care-i-gave-i-inspired/ba-p/13072159  - Marilyn Pratt talks about the community's giving  - although sadly many of the videos have been lost in space.

@JimSpath got us all started on our way to video fame with the SCNotty awards - 30 seconds or so to introduce ourselves to the community in some memorable ways. 

@MattHarding and the many talented members of the #sapteched Jam Band - showed that geeks can dance, but (some of them) can also sing, play guitar, and rock out!

I'll have to have a look at a different computer (the way-back machine) to see if I can dredge up some photos.  Meanwhile, thanks for all the memories!

Sue

entry.title: Re: O primeiro passo de uma boa jornada para o SAP S/4HANA
entry.content: Excelente conteúdo @Rodrigo_Cordeiro  ! Parabens! Por mais conteudos que agreguem e nos ajudem! #GoSAP #WeMakeSAPSolutionsHappen

entry.title: Re: Fiori launchpad access
entry.content: If you have checked with all necessary security permission with basis team then TCODE /n/ui2/flp works.

entry.title: Re: Getting Error while creating item
entry.content: Then submit a ticket to SAP Support.

entry.title: Re: Which function call returns 0? in sap abap
entry.content:
Dear Sushmithanaveenanthea,


ABAP has several functions that return 0.

Example 1:

Returns the length of a string as an integer (type i). If you call it with an empty string or an initial string variable, you will receive exactly 0.
DATA(lv_zero) = strlen( '' ). " lv_zero = 0

Example 2:

Returns the number of rows in an internal table as an integer. If the table is empty, the result is also 0.
DATA(lv_zero) = lines( itab_empty ). " lv_zero = 0

Example 3: Index Function for Table Expressions

If you access the table via an index that doesn't exist, the expression function returns 0:

DATA: lt_outer TYPE TABLE OF ty_inner,
lt_inner TYPE TABLE OF ty_line.
lt_outer = VALUE #( ( lt_inner ) ). "Outer table with an entry that itself contains an empty lt_inner table
WRITE / lt_outer[ 2 ]. "returns 0 because there is no second row in lt_outer

There are many more examples where 0 is returned.

I hope these examples will help you.

Best regards,
Dennis

entry.title: Re: Credit checks available in SAP Credit Management (FSCM)
entry.content: Can we have credit check on Sales orders which results only in proforma invoices? if yes the exposure will keep building - is there any way exposure do not keep on building for sales order types which result in proforma invoice.

entry.title: Re: Recuriting candidates' group mail
entry.content:
You can send emails to more than 10 candidates at once. First, increase the number of items per page using the dropdown menu at the bottom. Then, select all the candidates and click the Send Email button. You should be able to see all the selected candidates on the next page.

Thanks,

Sanjeet
